concerns.text: FR-007 requires sensitivity analysis sweeping cutoffs over {0.01, 0.05, 0.1}, but US-3 acceptance scenarios do not include any acceptance scenario validating this sensitivity analysis requirement. This creates a gap where a functional requirement exists without corresponding acceptance criteria.

FR-006 states 'flag collinearity when |r|≥0.7' but US-3 Acceptance Scenario 3 says 'VIF ≥5 is flagged'. These are different metrics (correlation coefficient vs. variance inflation factor). The spec should clarify whether |r|≥0.7 triggers VIF calculation, or if VIF≥5 is the actual flagging threshold.
